method_atom: The analysis of time-periodic, spatially localized solutions of the coupled nonlinear equations of motion for many interacting classical units arranged on a discrete network, focusing on the energy properties of such self-localized excitations: whether a positive lower bound on their energy exists depends on the network's spatial dimension, with the localizing lower bound appearing only above a critical dimension that can generically exceed two, alongside regimes where localized excitations persist at arbitrarily low energy in any dimension or arise without bifurcating from a delocalized extended (band-edge) mode.
object_atom: Discrete breathers, i.e. anharmonic localized vibrational excitations of a physical lattice of classical degrees of freedom (coupled anharmonic oscillator / crystal-lattice systems) in spatial dimension d, whose energy thresholds matter for their experimental detection.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the existence-and-energy-threshold machinery for self-localized periodic modes on a coupled-unit network as a function of its dimensionality; the domain-specific part is the concrete anharmonic physical lattice (discrete breathers) the analysis is applied to.
